spec.label: architect
spec.prompt:
You are designing 'Smile Today' — a beautiful, minimal mindfulness web app whose only job is to gently remind the user every hour to smile at someone real. Single-page, no accounts, no payment, no tracking. The user wants something he opens once, sets up, and lets quietly hum in the background.

Output a tight Markdown spec with EXACTLY these section headings, in this order, no others:

## Concept
2 sentences. The promise the app makes to the user. Slightly spiritual but never religious.

## Features
5–8 bullets. Each one is something the user actually sees or does. Keep it small.

## File structure
A tree of every file the static web app needs. Single-page, no build step. Suggested: index.html, style.css, script.js, logo.svg, manifest.json, sw.js.

## Notification flow
Step-by-step: Web Notifications API permission request copy, default interval (1 hour), allowed range (15 min – 4 h), how the Service Worker shows the notification when the tab is hidden, what happens on click (close notification, optional micro-animation in the open tab).

## On-screen copy
Every word the user reads, in English. Short, warm, intentional. Include: the headline, the timer label, the call-to-action button label, the settings labels, 10 short rotating quotes about smiling at strangers (one per line, ≤ 90 chars each).

## Color palette
Fixed three-color: pure black #0a0a0a, pure white #ffffff, one red accent. Pick the red and justify in one sentence.

## Type stack
Google Fonts pair (one display, one body). Justify the pairing.

## Settings stored in localStorage
List every key + value type + default.

Be opinionated. No 'depends on the user' hedging. The user is one specific person who wants to feel a little kinder every hour.

spec.label: designer
spec.prompt:
OUTPUT MODE: TEXT ONLY. Do not call any tools, do not invoke write_file, read_file, run_shell_command, sub-agent delegation. Respond with raw text in the format below, nothing else.

You are the lead designer (different vendor than the architect). From the spec, design the visual identity:

## Logo
A self-contained <svg viewBox='0 0 240 240'> ~240×240 px. Pure white background, the wordmark 'Smile Today' in black with ONE minimal red element — a single dot, a tiny curve, or a soft underline. NO emoji, NO smiley face cliché. Clean, intentional, slightly poetic. Output the full SVG markup ready to paste into a file.

## Color tokens
CSS custom-property block (--bg, --fg, --muted, --accent, --shadow, --radius-*, --space-*) using the architect's three colors plus subtle variants.

## Typography pairing
The two Google Fonts the architect picked, with their @import lines and base font-size scale (h1, h2, body, micro).

## Layout — three key states
For each: STATE NAME, purpose in 2 sentences, then a self-contained inline SVG (≥ 800×500) showing the actual layout with real labels, real button text, real countdown numbers.
  1. First-open (no permission yet)
  2. Active (notifications running, big countdown)
  3. Settings panel (sliders, toggles)

## Motion
4–6 specific animations (cubic-bezier curves, durations) used across the app — fade-ins, the hourly pulse on the countdown, the ripple after permission is granted. Be concrete.

## Mood
3 sentences. Calm, breathing, intentional. Never corporate, never cute.

## Spec
{{architect}}

spec.label: engineer
spec.prompt:
You are the implementer. Output the FULL working static web app — no framework, no build step, copy-pasteable into a folder and serve.

For every file, output a fenced code block whose FIRST line is a comment with the file path:
  - HTML files use `<!-- index.html -->`
  - CSS files use `/* style.css */`
  - JS files use `// script.js`
  - SVG files use `<!-- logo.svg -->`
  - JSON files use a leading line `<!-- manifest.json -->` then the JSON below

Required files:
  index.html, style.css, script.js, logo.svg, manifest.json, sw.js, robots.txt

Hard requirements:
  - Use the design tokens from the Designer step VERBATIM (CSS custom properties block as given).
  - Use the SVG logo from the Designer step VERBATIM (drop into logo.svg).
  - Implement the on-screen copy from the Architect step VERBATIM (10 rotating quotes, headline, button labels, settings labels).
  - Web Notifications API permission flow with the exact request copy from the spec.
  - Hourly notification scheduler running on the page (setInterval) and a Service Worker (sw.js) that shows notifications when the tab is hidden — registered from script.js.
  - Settings: interval (slider 15 min – 4 h, default 60 min), sound on/off toggle, all persisted to localStorage with the keys named in the spec.
  - Smooth fade-ins, the pulse animation specified in the design Motion section.
  - Fully mobile-responsive. PWA manifest with name 'Smile Today', short_name 'Smile', start_url '/', display 'standalone', theme_color = the red accent, background_color = #0a0a0a.
  - Beautiful big-breathing-room layout. Pure black bg, pure white fg, red accent only on the timer / CTA.
  - At least one subtle interaction surprise (e.g. when the timer hits zero before reset, the dot pulses; or holding the CTA button shows a soft secondary message).

No 'TODO' comments. No placeholder strings. No 'add real content here'. If anything is genuinely impossible without more info, state which file is partial AT THE BOTTOM after all complete files.

## Spec
{{architect}}

## Design
{{designer}}

spec.label: operator
spec.prompt:
OUTPUT MODE: TEXT ONLY. Do not call any tools (no write_file, read_file, run_shell_command, sub-agent delegation). Respond with raw text in the requested format.

Write the project's complete README + a one-line deploy command, no other docs.

Output two files as fenced blocks with file-path first-line comments:

  <!-- README.md -->
  Title 'Smile Today', a one-line tagline (≤12 words), 3-sentence what-it-does, '## Try it' section with the live URL placeholder `https://claudegravity.online`, '## Run locally' section (open index.html in a browser, that's it), 'Browser support: Chrome / Edge / Firefox / Safari 16+ for Web Notifications', license MIT one-liner.

  <!-- deploy.sh -->
  Single bash one-liner using rsync to upload to root@46.36.39.161:/var/www/smile-today/, plus the systemctl reload caddy line. Make it executable in the comment.

Keep it tight. Spec for context only:
## Spec
{{architect}}
